title: Pseudopodospermum crocifolium
intro: Pseudopodospermum crocifolium, also known as the crocus-leaved or yellow-flowered Podospermum, is a flowering plant species in the family Apiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Pseudopodospermum crocifolium include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which may contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and may also be used to make tinctures or capsules, often used to alleviate symptoms of fever and digestive discomfort.
taxonomy: Pseudopodospermum crocifolium belongs to the family Capparaceae. It is a plant species native to the Himalayan and Southeast Asian regions. The classification of Pseudopodospermum crocifolium is further divided into the genus Pseudopodospermum and the species P. crocifolium. The plant is a member of the order Brassicales within the clade Sapindales. Its classification is closely related to other plants in the family Capparaceae, such as Cleome and Capparis.
